content.problemStatement:
Chef is interested by buying a new phone, and is in the process of researching what's available on the market.

His favorite brand, Kitchen Telecom, just released their latest model.

Kitchen Telecom releases a new phone every year, and so names its phones based upon the year of release: the name of the phone model launched in year XXX is K\texttt{K}K (the letter) followed by the last two digits of XXX.

For instance, the phone model launched in the year 202420242024 is named K24\texttt{K24}K24.

The current year is XXX. What's the latest phone model launched by Kitchen Telecom?


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer XXX, the current year.


Output Format
Print the name of Kitchen Telecom's phone launched in year XXX.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1973 ≤ X ≤ 2024), the current year for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a separate line the model name of the phone released in year X. The model name is the character 'K' followed by the last two digits of X, preserving leading zeros.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For 2000, the last two digits are 00, so the model is K00. For 2024, the last two digits are 24, so the model is K24.

solution.approach: For each year X we need only its last two decimal digits. This can be obtained by X % 100. The result must be printed with exactly two digits, adding a leading zero when necessary. In most languages we can format the number with a width of 2 and fill character '0', or manually construct the string. The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
